title: Install one authoritative bee source and prove package release
action: Implement exclusive plugin-first and repo-copy distribution across both installers (D9-D14). Add one shared Node helper used by Bash/PowerShell to discover the installed bee package, prove it enabled, compare its complete canonical skills plus runtime hook payload against the release inventory, maintain an exact installer ownership ledger for user roots, preflight all cleanup targets, and apply only after every proof passes. Plugin-first removes only plain owned bee-* project skill directories and catalog-recognized bee hook entries from Claude/Codex project configuration while preserving every user entry/container; symlink, alias, file, unknown root, corrupt ledger, or inventory mismatch refuses the whole cleanup with zero mutation (D10, D11, D14). Repo-copy first disables/uninstalls the plugin and proves it inactive before onboarding vendors project skills/hooks; failure leaves the repo untouched (D12). Keep runtime modes explicit and idempotent. Expand release inventory to include canonical plugin skills, both hook payloads, manifests, and the shared distribution machinery; add executable fixture tests for dry-run/apply/repeat, plugin proof failure, user-content survival, hook cleanup, reverse transition, ledger failure, Bash/PowerShell parity, cachebuster/reinstall fixture, and source-checkout fallback distinction (D13). Add the new suite to the configured verify manifest without weakening any existing suite. Synchronize current docs/specs and prove the corrected worktree state/review routing remains unchanged. Actual public publication remains outside this cell.

truths: Plugin-first cleanup cannot start until the enabled installed package's complete skills/hooks inventory matches the release inventory.
A failed package, ledger, path, symlink, alias, or hook-container proof leaves all target bytes unchanged.
Plugin-first ends with no active project bee skill copies or recognized project bee hook entries, while all user/foreign content remains byte-identical.
Repo-copy refuses until the bee plugin is proven inactive and never leaves both sources active.
User-root deletion is authorized only by a valid exact installer ownership ledger.
Bash and PowerShell have equivalent mode, dry-run, cleanup, refusal, repeat, and reporting semantics.
Release inventory covers canonical plugin skills, Codex/Claude hooks, manifests, installers, and the distribution helper/tests.
One cachebuster/reinstall fixture and fresh-thread acceptance procedure prove package pickup; project fallback checks do not substitute.
The unchanged configured full repository verify includes the new distribution suite and exits zero before completion.

artifacts.substantive: shared strict package proof, source arbitration, ownership ledger, and zero-mutation cleanup engine
executable cross-runtime fixture matrix for both directions and destructive boundaries
explicit plugin-first/repo-copy orchestration using the shared helper
PowerShell orchestration behaviorally aligned with Bash
complete plugin payload and distribution inventory
current package authority, two-way arbitration, cleanup, and ownership rules
plugin versus fallback hook ownership and exactly-one-source behavior

key_links: both installers invoke the same helper for package proof, cleanup planning, apply, and ledger decisions
release inventory output is the exact comparison input used before cleanup
onboarding repo-copy path is reachable only after plugin-inactive proof
existing catalog bee-hook matchers preserve user hook entries during plugin-first cleanup
verify-manifest guard requires the new distribution suite in .bee/config.json commands.verify
prohibitions: Do not delete whole .claude, .agents, .codex, skills, settings, or hooks containers.
Do not authorize cleanup by basename or command exit alone.
Do not follow or delete symlinks, aliases, files, foreign skills, user hook entries, or outside-root targets.
Do not create repo copies while the plugin is active or clean fallbacks before installed-package integrity proof.
Do not hand-edit protected source-checkout .agents/.codex projections as plugin delivery.
Do not weaken or remove an existing verification suite.
Do not publish/push a public release or mutate worktree-isolation execution state.

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs && node scripts/test_portable_paths.mjs && node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs && node scripts/test_release_tuple.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_split_brain_regression.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --selftest && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/test_gate_bypass_doctrine.mjs

trace.outcome: Installed one strict shared distribution authority for plugin-first/repo-copy, safe cleanup, validated plugin metadata, and complete release proof
deviations: Auto-fixed a blocking upstream projection mismatch: repo-copy onboarding now renders the canonical Codex subagent audit hooks added by cell 2.
Real Windows PowerShell parser evidence was unavailable because WSL interop failed before PowerShell started; repository ASCII and fixture guards remained green.
trace.friction: unattributable failure: Windows PowerShell interop failed before parser startup
